message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Adding validate_nesting=False param to Context.__init__; tracking child contexts (e.g. self._children list) in __init__; validation logic in close()/__exit__; inheritance + child registration in _make_sub_context(); in shell_completion.py, setting validate_nesting=False explicitly in _resolve_context()'s make_context() calls

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrap _resolve_context() in shell_completion.py with 'with' statements (main ctx, sub_ctx for non-chained groups, sub_sub_ctx for chained groups) so contexts close properly. Also a docstring-only tweak to Context.exit() in core.py (no functional change). Please tell me which functions/classes in these two files your feature touches.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context() function
code: def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesnt trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False
    with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            args = sub_ctx.args
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
note: Merges my with-block resource-cleanup fix (upstream click PR #2800) with your validate_nesting=False flag on all 3 make_context() calls. Confirm the kwarg name/spelling is right for your Context.__init__ signature, and that passing it via ctx_args dict for the top-level call + explicit kwarg for sub calls works for you.

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context()
note: My feature (validate_nesting) needs every make_context()/Context() call inside _resolve_context to explicitly pass validate_nesting=False (so shell completion never trips RuntimeError from unclosed-children validation). This overlaps with your with-statement wrapping fix. Since it's your fix's territory, please send a DRAFT of the full merged _resolve_context() body with 'with' wrapping AND validate_nesting=False added to each make_context call (top-level ctx_args, non-chain make_context, and chained sub_ctx make_context). I'll compare against your version. No overlap expected on Context.exit() since your change there is docstring-only.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context() function
code: def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False
    with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        args = sub_ctx._protected_args + sub_ctx.args
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            args = sub_sub_ctx.args
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
note: Fixed a bug from your draft: in both with-blocks, 'args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args' and 'args = sub_ctx.args' referenced the OLD (pre-reassignment) context instead of the newly-created one (sub_ctx / sub_sub_ctx respectively) -- that would make the loop reuse stale/already-consumed args instead of the new sub-context's leftover args. Fixed to reference the new context before/without needing the reassignment order to matter. Also restored the docstring apostrophe (doesn't) -- assuming that was just quoting-transport mangling on your end, not an intended edit. Everything else identical to your draft (with-wrapping + validate_nesting=False on all 3 make_context calls). Please confirm this exact text.

code: def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],

    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and

                        ctx = sub_ctx
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx
                            args = sub_ctx.args

                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
note: Good catch on the ordering bug. I checked the real pallets/click release wheels: click 8.2.0 shipped with the stale-ctx bug you spotted, and click 8.2.1 fixed it by reordering to 'ctx = sub_ctx' THEN 'args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args' (same fix as yours, just reordering instead of renaming to sub_ctx explicitly - functionally identical). I used the exact 8.2.1 wording for canonical fidelity. Docstring apostrophe restored too - that was transport mangling on my end, not intentional. Please confirm this exact text and I'll send AGREE.

note: Confirmed, locking this in. Note: my core.py edit is a docstring-only change to Context.exit() (around the versionchanged 8.2 note), which is textually separate from your __init__/close/__exit__/_make_sub_context work, so no further coordination needed there unless you touch that exact docstring.

type: DONE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: Implemented the agreed _resolve_context() verbatim in shell_completion.py, plus my non-overlapping docstring-only tweak to Context.exit() in core.py. As expected, tests/test_shell_completion.py now fails with TypeError: Context.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'validate_nesting' until your Context.__init__/close/__exit__ changes land - that's the expected intermediate state per our split. Let me know when your core.py changes are in so I can re-verify the full suite.

Verified: full test suite passes (558 passed, tests/test_context.py + tests/test_shell_completion.py both green), plus a manual sanity check that validate_nesting=True raises RuntimeError on closing a parent with open children and works fine when False (default). You're clear to re-verify on your end.
